tasks.title: Design and implement the Missing Writer main quest narrative framework
tasks.description: Replace the current generic 6-chapter main quest progression with a themed narrative: the player is a reporter from abroad investigating the mysterious disappearance of a famous writer from the target-language-speaking country. (1) In shared/quest/main-quest-chapters.ts, rewrite the 6 chapters with the new narrative: Chapter 1 'Assignment Abroad' (A1) — player arrives in the foreign land, learns basic greetings, visits the local newspaper office for their first lead; Chapter 2 'Following the Trail' (A1) — player explores the town, talks to locals who knew the writer, collects the writer's first book from the local library/bookshop; Chapter 3 'The Inner Circle' (A2) — player befriends the writer's associates (editor, neighbor, patron), discovers the writer was researching something controversial, collects more books/documents; Chapter 4 'Hidden Messages' (A2) — player discovers coded messages in the writer's books, follows clues to locations outside the main settlement, talks to people in other areas; Chapter 5 'The Truth Emerges' (B1) — player pieces together the mystery using advanced language skills, debates with scholars about the writer's work, discovers the writer went into hiding voluntarily; Chapter 6 'The Final Chapter' (B2) — player locates the writer, has a culminating conversation in the target language, writes and files their report. (2) Update narrative beat intro/outro text for each chapter with evocative story moments. (3) Each chapter should have 4-6 objectives mixing quest types (conversation, vocabulary, collection, exploration, photography) that tie into the mystery. (4) The writer's name should be procedurally generated based on the target language (French → a French name, Spanish → a Spanish name, etc.). (5) Update GameMenuSystem.ts journal rendering to show the mystery narrative context — chapter descriptions should read like investigation notes.

tasks.title: Add a Texts schema, collection, and CRUD endpoints for procedurally generated reading content
tasks.description: Create a full data model for Texts — reading content that can be procedurally generated or hand-authored. (1) Add a 'texts' table to shared/schema.ts with fields: id, worldId, title (target language), titleTranslation (English), textCategory ('book'|'journal'|'letter'|'flyer'|'recipe'), pages (JSON array of { content: string, contentTranslation: string }), vocabularyHighlights (JSON array of { word, translation, partOfSpeech }), comprehensionQuestions (JSON array of { question, questionTranslation, options: string[], correctIndex }), cefrLevel ('A1'|'A2'|'B1'|'B2'), targetLanguage, authorName (in-world author, e.g., the missing writer), clueText (optional — what this text reveals for the main quest), difficulty ('beginner'|'intermediate'|'advanced'), tags (string array), isGenerated (boolean — AI-generated vs hand-authored), generationPrompt (the prompt used to generate it, for regeneration), spawnLocationHint (where in the world this text should appear: 'library', 'bookshop', 'cafe', 'residence', 'office', 'hidden', 'market', etc.), status ('draft'|'published'), createdAt, updatedAt. (2) Add a Mongoose model in mongo-storage.ts with all CRUD operations: createText, getTextsByWorld, getTextById, updateText, deleteText, getTextsByCategory, getTextsByCefrLevel. (3) Add REST endpoints in server/routes.ts: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/worlds/:worldId/texts, plus GET /api/worlds/:worldId/texts?category=book&cefrLevel=A1 for filtering. (4) Add a batch generation endpoint: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/texts/generate that accepts { count, category, cefrLevel, targetLanguage, theme } and uses the Gemini API to generate multiple texts at once.

tasks.title: Implement procedural text generation using Gemini API
tasks.description: Build the AI text generation pipeline for creating reading content in the target language. (1) Create server/services/text-generator.ts with a generateText() function that calls the Gemini API with a carefully crafted prompt. The prompt should specify: target language, CEFR level, text category (book/journal/letter/flyer/recipe), desired page count (1-4), word count per page (100-200), theme/topic, and optionally a clue to weave in (for main quest texts). (2) The prompt must instruct Gemini to: write ONLY in the target language at the specified CEFR level, use vocabulary appropriate to that level (A1 = 500 most common words, A2 = 1000, B1 = 2000, B2 = unrestricted), include natural-feeling prose (not textbook language), and return structured JSON with pages, vocabulary highlights (10-15 key words per text with translations), and 3-5 comprehension questions with answer options. (3) Category-specific prompts: 'book' = literary excerpt (fiction, memoir, poetry), 'journal' = personal diary entries with dated headers, 'letter' = correspondence with salutation/closing, 'flyer' = advertisement or announcement with bold/emphatic language, 'recipe' = cooking instructions with ingredient lists and steps. (4) Add difficulty scaling: A1 texts use present tense only, short sentences, common vocabulary; A2 adds past tense and basic connectors; B1 adds subjunctive, conditionals, idiomatic expressions; B2 uses literary language, metaphor, complex grammar. (5) For language-learning worlds, auto-generate a starter set during world creation: 12 books (3 per CEFR level), 4 journals (1 per level), 4 letters (1 per level), 4 flyers (1 per level), 4 recipes (1 per level) = 28 texts total. (6) For the Missing Writer main quest, generate the 12 books with clue text tied to each chapter. (7) Validate generated content: check page count, word count, ensure comprehension questions have valid correctIndex, ensure vocabulary highlights actually appear in the text. Regenerate if validation fails. (8) Cache generated content in the texts collection so it persists across sessions.

tasks.title: Add a Texts section to the Content editor for managing and generating texts
tasks.description: Add a new 'Texts' tab to the Admin Panel alongside Worlds, Assets, Items, Rules, Actions. (1) Create client/src/components/admin/AdminTextsHub.tsx following the pattern of AdminRulesActionsHub.tsx with a three-panel layout: left panel = text list with category filters (book/journal/letter/flyer/recipe) and CEFR level filters; center panel = text preview showing the full content with syntax highlighting for vocabulary words; right panel = metadata (title, category, CEFR level, target language, author, tags, spawn location hint, generation prompt). (2) Add a 'Generate Text' button that opens a dialog with fields: category dropdown, CEFR level dropdown, target language, theme/topic text field, page count slider (1-4), optional clue text. On submit, call POST /api/worlds/:worldId/texts/generate and show a loading spinner. When generation completes, display the result for review before saving. (3) Add a 'Generate Batch' button that generates multiple texts at once: specify count per category and CEFR level. Useful for quickly populating a new world with reading content. (4) Add inline editing: creators can click on any text to edit its title, pages, vocabulary, comprehension questions, and clue text directly. Changes save via PUT endpoint. (5) Add a 'Regenerate' button per text that re-runs the Gemini generation using the stored generationPrompt, replacing the current content. (6) Add a 'Preview as Player' mode that shows the text exactly as it would appear in the in-game Library (with hover-to-translate, page navigation, and quiz). (7) Show text status badges: 'Draft' (yellow), 'Published' (green). Only published texts appear in the game world. (8) Add the Texts tab to AdminPanel.tsx tab list alongside the existing 5 tabs.

tasks.title: Source 3D assets for each text category and place collectible texts in the world
tasks.description: Each text category needs a distinct 3D model so players can visually distinguish them. (1) Find or create 3D assets for each category: 'book' = closed book model (brown leather look), 'journal' = small notebook/diary, 'letter' = envelope or folded paper, 'flyer' = flat poster/broadsheet, 'recipe' = rolled parchment or card. Check Polyhaven and Sketchfab for free CC0/CC-BY models. Save to client/public/assets/models/props/. (2) Register each as a base item with appropriate objectRole: 'text_book', 'text_journal', 'text_letter', 'text_flyer', 'text_recipe'. (3) Create client/src/components/3DGame/TextSpawner.ts that reads published texts from the API and places them in the world at appropriate locations based on spawnLocationHint: 'library' → inside library building, 'bookshop' → inside bookshop on shelves, 'cafe' → on a cafe table, 'residence' → inside a residence on desk/table, 'office' → in the newspaper/city hall office, 'market' → on a market stall, 'hidden' → in a harder-to-find outdoor location. (4) Each text mesh should have a subtle glow/particle effect (golden for books, blue for letters, green for recipes) and float slightly with a gentle bob animation. (5) When player presses E near a text, collect it: remove from world, add to player's Library collection, show notification with the text's title and category icon. (6) Main quest texts (the writer's books) should only spawn when the relevant chapter is active. Side content (recipes, flyers, etc.) can spawn from the start. (7) Use the text's category to select the correct 3D model. (8) Register text collection with the quest system — fire 'text_collected' event on GameEventBus with { textId, category, cefrLevel }.

tasks.title: Overhaul the Library menu page to display all text categories with reading interface
tasks.description: The Library tab in GameMenuSystem.ts currently shows notice board articles with translations visible by default. Completely overhaul it: (1) Replace the flat article list with a categorized bookshelf layout. Show category tabs at the top: Books (📕), Journals (📓), Letters (✉️), Flyers (📄), Recipes (🍳), Notices (📌). Each tab shows collected items of that type as clickable thumbnails/spines. Uncollected items for the main quest show as grayed-out silhouettes with '???' to hint at their existence. (2) Clicking any text opens a dedicated reading view: show one page at a time with left/right page navigation arrows. Display ONLY the target-language text by default — NO English translation visible. (3) Implement hover-to-translate for individual words: when the player hovers over a word, show a tooltip with English translation, part of speech, and pronunciation guide. Use vocabularyHighlights for known translations; for other words, use dictionary lookup from the vocabulary corpus. (4) Highlighted vocabulary words should be visually distinct (underlined or slightly different color). (5) At the end of each text (after last page), show a comprehension quiz: questions one at a time, multiple choice, in the target language. Track answers. (6) Persist reading progress and quiz completion per text to the server. (7) Award XP for reading: +5 XP per new page read, +10-25 XP per correct quiz answer (scaled by CEFR level). (8) Show progress per text: 'Read: 3/4 pages, Quiz: 2/3 correct'. (9) Category-specific visual treatment: books get a leather-bound page frame, letters get an envelope/stationery frame, recipes get a kitchen-card frame, flyers get a bulletin-board pin frame. (10) Apply the same hover-to-translate treatment to existing notice board articles instead of the show/hide toggle.

tasks.title: Implement asset scaling management in the Admin Panel Assets manager
tasks.description: Currently some building and prop assets are way too big or too small (e.g., a food stand as tall as a 2-story building, ground texture leaves 3x taller than NPCs). Add scaling controls. (1) Add a 'scale' field to the visual_assets metadata in shared/schema.ts: scale: { x: number, y: number, z: number } with default { x: 1, y: 1, z: 1 }. Also add a 'textureScale' field: number (default 1.0) for controlling texture tiling repeat. (2) In AdminAssetsHub.tsx, add a 'Scale' section to the asset detail view. Show three number inputs (X, Y, Z scale) with 0.01 step increments and a 'Uniform Scale' checkbox that locks all three axes together (most common use case). Add a texture scale slider for texture assets (0.1x to 10x). (3) Add an NPC reference preview: when editing an asset's scale, show a 3D preview panel that renders the asset alongside a reference NPC model (standard humanoid capsule, ~1.8m tall). The user can rotate the preview to see the asset from all angles. This immediately shows whether the asset is the right size relative to a human. Add grid lines at 1m intervals on the ground plane for additional reference. (4) The preview should update in real-time as the user adjusts scale sliders — drag the slider and see the asset grow/shrink next to the NPC. (5) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts and BabylonGame.ts, when loading any asset, read the stored scale from the asset's metadata and apply it via mesh.scaling.set(scale.x, scale.y, scale.z) BEFORE any other scaling logic (like story-based height). (6) For texture assets, apply textureScale to the texture's uScale and vScale properties when loading ground/wall/roof textures. (7) Add a 'Reset Scale' button that reverts to { x: 1, y: 1, z: 1 }. (8) Add a 'Fit to Stories' helper button for building assets that auto-calculates the scale needed to make the asset match a given number of stories (input 1, 2, or 3) based on the standard story height (3.5m). (9) Save scale changes via PUT /api/assets/:id with the updated metadata. (10) Add bulk scale operations: select multiple assets and apply the same scale factor to all of them.

tasks.title: Implement hover-to-translate for all target-language text in the game
tasks.description: The current Library shows full English translations by default, which gives learners the answer. Replace this pattern everywhere with hover-to-translate. (1) Create a reusable InteractiveTextBlock utility in client/src/components/3DGame/InteractiveTextBlock.ts that wraps Babylon.js GUI TextBlock but supports per-word hover detection. Since Babylon.js GUI doesn't natively support per-word hover, implement this by: splitting text into individual word TextBlocks laid out in a flow layout, each word being a separate clickable control. (2) On hover/click of a word: show a floating tooltip panel with translation, part of speech, example sentence. Add a small delay (200ms) to prevent accidental triggers. (3) Apply InteractiveTextBlock to: Library text pages, Library notice articles, NPC chat messages (target-language portions), sign text, quest descriptions that include target-language text. (4) For the NPC chat panel, this is especially important — when an NPC speaks in French, the player should be able to hover over unfamiliar words to see translations without switching to a full-translation mode. (5) Track which words the player looks up — this feeds into vocabulary learning. If a player hovers over a word for the first time, add it to their vocabulary as 'new'. If they look it up again later, it confirms they're still learning it. (6) Add a visual indicator on words the player has already mastered (e.g., no underline) vs words they're still learning (dotted underline) vs new words (solid underline). (7) Ensure this doesn't break the existing translation toggle — the toggle should switch between 'hover mode' (default) and 'full translation visible' for accessibility.

tasks.title: Persist Library reading progress and quiz completion to the server
tasks.description: Library quiz progress is currently stored only in an in-memory Set and resets on page reload. Fix: (1) Define a ReadingProgress type in shared/language/progress.ts: { textId: string, textCategory: string, pagesRead: number[], quizAnswers: { questionIndex: number, selectedAnswer: number, correct: boolean }[], completedAt?: string, xpAwarded: number }. (2) Add a readingProgress array to the LanguageProgress type. (3) Add server storage: implement upsertReadingProgress() in mongo-storage.ts that saves/updates reading progress per player per world. (4) Add API endpoints: POST /api/language-progress/:playerId/:worldId/reading — save reading progress; GET to retrieve. (5) In GameMenuSystem.ts Library tab, on page turn save progress immediately (debounced 2s). On quiz answer, save immediately. (6) On Library tab open, load saved progress and restore state: previously read pages shown as 'read', answered quiz questions shown with the player's previous answer and whether it was correct. (7) Show a completion badge on fully-read texts with all quiz questions answered correctly. (8) Track total texts read by category and quiz accuracy in the Language Progress overview. (9) Also persist notice board article reading and quiz completion using the same system — replace the in-memory answeredNoticeQuestions Set with server-persisted data.

tasks.title: Fix vocabulary tracking to capture all words encountered in NPC conversations
tasks.description: LanguageProgressTracker.ts currently only tracks vocabulary from a hardcoded sampleWords list, missing most words the player encounters. Fix: (1) Replace the sampleWords-only extraction in analyzeNPCResponse() with a comprehensive approach: tokenize the NPC's target-language response into individual words, look up each word against shared/language/language-vocabulary-corpus.ts (which should contain a comprehensive French vocabulary dictionary). (2) If language-vocabulary-corpus.ts doesn't have a large enough dictionary, expand it: add the 2000 most common French words with English translations, categories, and CEFR levels. Source from standard frequency lists. (3) For each recognized word in NPC speech that the player hasn't encountered before, add it to the player's vocabulary as 'new'. For words they've seen before, increment timesEncountered. (4) For words the player USES in their own messages, check them against known vocabulary and increment timesUsedCorrectly if the word is used in a valid context (simple check: the word exists in the target language dictionary). (5) Update masteryLevel transitions: new → learning (after 3 encounters), learning → familiar (after 5 correct uses), familiar → mastered (after 10 correct uses AND accuracy >= 80%). (6) Fire onWordLearned and onWordMastered callbacks properly so the gamification tracker can award XP. (7) Show a subtle toast when a new word is learned: 'New word: bonjour (hello)' with a small +XP indicator.

tasks.title: Fix grammar tracking by adding structured grammar feedback to NPC responses
tasks.description: Grammar tracking infrastructure exists but NPC responses never include the structured **GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK** blocks that LanguageProgressTracker expects. Fix: (1) In the NPC conversation system prompt (likely in server/services/context-manager.ts or wherever Gemini API prompts are constructed), add an instruction telling the AI to include grammar feedback when the player makes grammatical errors in the target language. The instruction should say: 'When the player makes a grammar mistake in [target language], include a feedback block at the end of your response in this exact format: **GRAMMAR_FEEDBACK** pattern: [pattern name] | correction: [corrected text] | explanation: [brief explanation in English] **END_GRAMMAR**'. (2) Define the standard grammar patterns to track in shared/language/grammar-patterns.ts: verb_conjugation, gender_agreement, article_usage, adjective_placement, preposition_usage, tense_consistency, pronoun_usage, negation_form, question_formation, plural_formation. Each with a description and common examples. (3) In LanguageProgressTracker.ts, ensure parseGrammarFeedbackBlock() is called on every NPC response and that results flow to recordGrammarFeedback(). (4) When a grammar pattern is mastered (10+ correct, 80%+ accuracy), fire onGrammarPatternMastered callback and award XP. (5) Display grammar corrections inline in the chat panel: highlight the corrected phrase with a colored underline, and show the explanation in a tooltip on hover. (6) In BabylonVocabularyPanel.ts Grammar tab, verify that tracked patterns display correctly with accuracy bars and example corrections.

tasks.title: Implement server-side persistence for vocabulary and grammar progress
tasks.description: The language-progress sync endpoint exists but the server storage methods are not implemented, so progress is lost on reload. Fix: (1) In server/db/mongo-storage.ts, implement upsertLanguageProgress(playerId, worldId, data): upsert a document in a 'language_progress' collection with the full LanguageProgress object. (2) Implement getLanguageProgress(playerId, worldId): retrieve the stored progress. (3) Implement upsertVocabularyEntries(playerId, worldId, entries): merge new vocabulary entries with existing ones (update encounter counts, mastery levels for existing words; insert new words). (4) Implement upsertGrammarPatterns(playerId, worldId, patterns): merge grammar pattern data similarly. (5) In the telemetry-routes.ts POST /api/language-progress/sync handler, call these storage methods instead of failing silently. (6) In the GET endpoint, return the stored data correctly. (7) On the client side in BabylonGame.ts, add periodic sync (every 60 seconds) and sync on game exit/pause: collect current vocabulary, grammar patterns, and conversation stats from LanguageProgressTracker and POST to the sync endpoint. (8) On game load, fetch existing progress from the server and hydrate the tracker with previously learned words and grammar patterns so the player doesn't lose progress. (9) Add playthroughId scoping so progress is per-playthrough, not just per-world.

tasks.title: Fix the Language Progress page to actually display vocabulary and grammar data
tasks.description: The Language Progress page in the game menu should show the player's vocabulary and grammar progress clearly. Investigate and fix: (1) Check if GameMenuSystem.ts has a Language Progress tab — if not, add one. It should show: total words learned, words by mastery level (new/learning/familiar/mastered as a stacked bar chart), total grammar patterns tracked with mastery status, overall fluency percentage, CEFR level, conversation count, streak days. (2) Verify that BabylonVocabularyPanel.ts (V key) actually receives data from LanguageProgressTracker — if the tracker's vocabulary array is empty because tracking is broken (see vocabulary fix task), the panel will show nothing. Wire the data flow: tracker → panel. (3) Add a vocabulary growth chart: simple line showing words learned over time (track daily totals). (4) Add grammar accuracy overview: for each tracked pattern, show name, accuracy %, mastery status, and a mini progress bar. (5) Add 'Words Due for Review' section using the spaced repetition logic in getWordsDueForReview() — show words the player should practice. (6) Add a 'Recent Activity' feed showing: last 5 words learned, last 3 grammar corrections, last conversation summary. (7) Ensure the Language Progress page loads data from the server (persisted progress) not just from the current session. (8) Link vocabulary entries to the Library — if a word was first learned from a text, show which text introduced it.

tasks.title: Seed texts for world 69bba18ce2e6d5c1cb3c7a9f using procedural generation
tasks.description: Use the text generation pipeline to populate the current game world with reading content. (1) Call the POST /api/worlds/69bba18ce2e6d5c1cb3c7a9f/texts/generate endpoint (or run text-generator.ts directly) to generate the starter set: 12 books for the Missing Writer main quest (3 per CEFR level, each containing a clue about the disappearance), 4 journals (personal diary entries from townspeople, 1 per CEFR level), 4 letters (correspondence between characters, 1 per level), 4 flyers (market announcements, festival notices, missing person poster for the writer, business advertisements), 4 recipes (local cuisine from the settlement's culture). (2) For the 12 main quest books, set clueText for each: book 1 = writer mentions a favorite garden, book 2 = references a disagreement with a patron, book 3 = describes a secret meeting place, etc., building toward the revelation that the writer went into hiding. Set spawnLocationHint appropriately: books 1-3 at library/bookshop/cafe, books 4-6 at newspaper/residence/school, books 7-9 at church/city hall/hidden outdoor spots, books 10-12 at increasingly remote/secretive locations. (3) For non-quest texts, set spawn locations contextually: recipes at restaurants/bakeries, flyers at notice boards/market, journals at residences, letters at post office/residences. (4) All texts should be in French (the world's target language). (5) Mark all as status='published'. (6) Verify the generated content: check that CEFR scaling feels right (A1 texts should be genuinely simple, B2 should be challenging), vocabulary highlights are accurate, comprehension questions are answerable from the text. (7) Write the seed as a runnable migration/script that can be re-run if content needs regeneration.

tasks.title: Wire text collection into main quest chapter progression
tasks.description: The writer's texts should be key progression items in the main quest. (1) In main-quest-chapters.ts, add a 'collect_evidence' objective type to each chapter: Chapter 1 requires collecting book 1, Chapter 2 requires books 2-3, Chapter 3 requires books 4-5, etc. (2) In main-quest-progression.ts recordQuestCompletion(), add handling for text collection events: when a text is collected, check if it satisfies the current chapter's collect_evidence objective. (3) Add a 'clue board' or 'investigation notes' section to the Journal tab: as the player collects texts and reads them, the clue text from each text is added to a running investigation summary. This gives the player a sense of piecing together the mystery. (4) Each chapter's narrative beat intro should reference the clues discovered so far: 'After reading [Writer]'s journal about the secret garden, you realize there may be more to discover...' (5) The final chapter should only unlock after the player has collected AND read all books (not just collected — they must read the pages and answer quiz questions). (6) Add a 'Clues Discovered: X/12' counter to the Journal page. (7) When all clues are assembled, trigger a special 'eureka' moment with a narrative beat: the player realizes where the writer is hiding.

tasks.title: Create main quest NPCs: the writer's associates who provide leads and context
tasks.description: The mystery needs key NPCs the player interacts with. (1) During world generation for language-learning worlds, create 4-6 special NPCs tied to the main quest: the_editor (works at a bookshop or newspaper — gives initial assignment and chapter 1-2 leads), the_neighbor (lives near the writer's last known address — gives personal details and chapter 2-3 leads), the_patron (wealthy character who funded the writer — gives financial clues and chapter 3-4 leads), the_scholar (works at library or school — discusses the writer's work and chapter 4-5 leads), the_confidant (writer's closest friend — provides the final crucial clue for chapter 5-6). (2) Tag these NPCs with 'main_quest_npc' and assign them to appropriate businesses/residences. (3) Give them unique conversation contexts: when the player talks to them, the NPC should reference the missing writer and share information relevant to the current main quest chapter. (4) Show quest indicators (!) on these NPCs when the current chapter involves talking to them. (5) Their dialogue should naturally incorporate vocabulary relevant to the current CEFR level. (6) The NPCs should have personality traits that color their perspective on the writer (the editor is professional and factual, the neighbor is gossipy and informal, the patron is formal and evasive, the scholar is analytical, the confidant is emotional). (7) Add these NPCs to the procedural generation pipeline so they're created consistently for every language-learning world.

tasks.title: Add main quest entries as proper quest records in the quest system
tasks.description: Currently main quest chapters track progress through a separate MainQuestState system but don't create actual quest entries visible in the quest list alongside side quests. Fix: (1) When a main quest chapter becomes active, create a proper Quest record in the database with: title = chapter title (e.g., 'Chapter 1: Assignment Abroad'), description = chapter narrative intro, questType = 'main_quest', status = 'active', objectives = the chapter's objectives converted to standard quest objective format, tags = ['main_quest', 'chapter:1'], locationPosition pointing to the first objective's target location. (2) Main quest entries should appear at the TOP of the quest tracker UI, visually distinguished (golden border, larger text, or a special icon). (3) When a chapter's objectives are completed, the quest status changes to 'completed' and the next chapter's quest is created. (4) Main quest objectives should generate minimap markers just like side quest objectives. (5) In the quest tracker overlay (during gameplay), show the active main quest prominently above side quests. (6) Ensure main quest completion awards the chapter's bonus XP (300-2000) AND gold rewards (50-500g scaling with chapter). (7) The quest description should include narrative context that feels like the player's investigation notes, not a generic quest log.

tasks.title: Enhance the Journal tab to show investigation progress with clue board
tasks.description: The Journal tab in GameMenuSystem.ts should feel like a reporter's investigation board. (1) Redesign the Journal layout with three sections: 'Investigation Board' (top — visual summary of clues and connections), 'Chapter Progress' (middle — current chapter objectives with progress bars), 'Case Notes' (bottom — scrollable log of story beats and discoveries). (2) The Investigation Board section should show: the writer's name and portrait (generated), a timeline of events ('Last seen: [date]', 'Book 3 found: [location]', etc.), collected evidence count (texts and conversations), key NPCs met with brief notes. (3) When new clues are discovered (text collected, key NPC conversation completed), animate the clue being added to the board with a brief highlight. (4) Chapter Progress should show the current chapter prominently with a progress bar and each objective listed with checkmark/pending status. Completed chapters shown as collapsed with a completion badge. (5) Case Notes should be a reverse-chronological log: 'Day 5 — Found the writer's third journal at the bookshop. It mentions a garden on the eastern hills...', 'Day 3 — Spoke with the editor. He seemed nervous when asked about the writer's final manuscript.' (6) Generate case note entries automatically when main quest events occur. Store them in the main quest state.

tasks.title: Apply stored asset scales throughout the rendering pipeline
tasks.description: The scale values saved in the asset manager need to be respected everywhere assets are loaded. (1) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts, when loading a building model from an asset, read the asset's metadata.scale and apply it as the BASE scale before any story-based scaling. If an asset has scale { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, z: 0.5 }, apply that first, then apply story multipliers on top. (2) In BabylonGame.ts, when loading prop/item models via objectModelTemplates, apply the asset's metadata.scale to the template mesh. All clones will inherit this scale. (3) For ground/wall/roof textures, read metadata.textureScale and set texture.uScale = texture.vScale = textureScale. This fixes the oversized leaf texture problem. (4) In BuildingInteriorGenerator.ts, when loading furniture models, apply stored scale. (5) In TextSpawner.ts and any other systems that spawn world objects, apply stored scale. (6) Add a migration (server/migrations/026-set-default-asset-scales.ts) that adds default scale { x: 1, y: 1, z: 1 } metadata to all existing assets that don't have it. (7) For known problematic assets (the oversized food stand, the giant leaf texture), set corrected scale values in the migration. (8) Ensure scale is loaded with the asset collection data so it's available client-side without extra API calls — include scale in the asset data returned by GET /api/worlds/:worldId/assets.

tasks.title: Add vocabulary word lookup tracking from hover-to-translate interactions
tasks.description: When the player uses hover-to-translate on any word (in texts, NPC chat, signs, etc.), track it as a vocabulary learning event. (1) In InteractiveTextBlock.ts (the hover-to-translate system), when a word tooltip is shown, fire a 'word_looked_up' event on GameEventBus with { word, translation, source ('book'|'journal'|'letter'|'flyer'|'recipe'|'chat'|'sign'|'notice'), context (surrounding sentence) }. (2) In LanguageProgressTracker.ts, listen for 'word_looked_up' events. If the word is not yet in the player's vocabulary, add it as 'new' with timesEncountered=1. If it already exists, increment timesEncountered. (3) Track lookup frequency: if a player looks up the same word 3+ times, it might indicate difficulty — flag it in the vocabulary panel as 'needs practice'. (4) In the Library reading view, show a 'Words Looked Up' count per reading session. (5) Award small XP for first-time word lookups: +1 XP per new word discovered through hover. (6) In the vocabulary panel, add a 'Source' column showing where the word was first encountered (e.g., 'Book: Le Jardin Secret', 'NPC: Marie Dupont', 'Sign: Boulangerie'). (7) This creates a natural learning loop: encounter word → look up → see in vocabulary panel → encounter again → recognize without lookup → mastered.

tasks.title: Add spaced repetition review prompts and vocabulary practice mini-games
tasks.description: The spaced repetition logic (getWordsDueForReview) exists but is never used. Wire it up: (1) Add a 'Practice' tab to BabylonVocabularyPanel.ts that shows words due for review. Display each word in the target language and ask the player to recall/type the English meaning (or vice versa). Simple flashcard format. (2) Show 5-10 words per practice session. After the player answers, show correct/incorrect feedback. Update the word's mastery level and next review time. (3) Award XP for practice sessions: +2 XP per correct answer, +5 XP for completing a full session. (4) Integrate with NPC conversations: when the player talks to an NPC, use getReviewWordsForNPC() to suggest the NPC incorporate words the player is learning into their dialogue. Pass these words to the Gemini API prompt as vocabulary the NPC should try to use naturally. (5) Add a daily vocabulary challenge: '5 words to review today' notification shown at the start of each game day. Completing the challenge gives a streak bonus. (6) In the Language Progress overview, show review stats: words reviewed today, streak days, mastery rate.

tasks.title: Add a French vocabulary corpus with 2000+ common words for comprehensive tracking
tasks.description: Vocabulary tracking needs a comprehensive dictionary to identify words in NPC speech. (1) Create or expand shared/language/french-vocabulary-corpus.ts with the 2000 most common French words, each with: word, english translation, partOfSpeech (noun/verb/adjective/adverb/preposition/conjunction/pronoun/article/interjection), gender (for nouns: masculine/feminine), cefrLevel (A1/A2/B1/B2), category (greetings, numbers, food, family, colors, time, weather, body, emotions, travel, shopping, school, work, nature, house, clothing, sports, health, technology, culture), frequency rank, example sentence in French, example translation. (2) Source the word list from established French frequency lists (e.g., Routledge frequency dictionary, Wiktionary frequency lists). (3) Add a lookup function: lookupWord(word: string, language: string): VocabularyCorpusEntry | null that handles: case-insensitive matching, common conjugation forms (parle → parler), accented character normalization (e/é/è/ê), plural forms (livres → livre). (4) Add a getWordsByLevel(cefrLevel: string): VocabularyCorpusEntry[] function for filtering. (5) This corpus powers: hover-to-translate tooltips, vocabulary tracking from NPC conversations, text word highlighting, quiz generation, and spaced repetition content. (6) Make the system extensible for other languages: define a LanguageCorpus interface that any language can implement.

tasks.title: Add grammar pattern definitions with explanations and examples for French
tasks.description: Grammar tracking needs well-defined patterns to track against. (1) Create shared/language/french-grammar-patterns.ts defining 15-20 key grammar patterns for French learners, organized by CEFR level. A1: article_usage (le/la/les/un/une/des), gender_agreement (adjective matches noun gender), basic_conjugation (present tense regular -er/-ir/-re verbs), negation (ne...pas), question_formation (est-ce que, inversion). A2: past_tense_passe_compose (avoir/être + participle), imparfait_vs_passe_compose, object_pronouns (le/la/les/lui/leur), reflexive_verbs (se + verb), preposition_usage (à/de/en/dans). B1: subjunctive_mood (que + subjunctive triggers), conditional_tense, relative_pronouns (qui/que/dont/où), passive_voice, adverb_placement. B2: literary_tenses (passé simple), complex_subjunctive, idiomatic_expressions, formal_register. (2) Each pattern should have: id, name, cefrLevel, description, rule explanation (in English), 3-5 correct examples, 3-5 common error examples with corrections. (3) This data feeds into: grammar tracking in LanguageProgressTracker, NPC grammar feedback generation (telling Gemini which patterns to watch for), grammar tab display in BabylonVocabularyPanel, and quiz generation.

tasks.title: Add NPC conversation prompts that incorporate vocabulary review and grammar focus
tasks.description: NPCs should naturally use vocabulary the player is learning and gently correct grammar. (1) In the conversation system prompt sent to Gemini, add dynamic context: 'The player is learning French at [CEFR level]. They are currently practicing these vocabulary words: [list of 5-10 words due for review from spaced repetition]. Try to naturally use some of these words in your response. The player has been making errors with these grammar patterns: [list of 2-3 patterns with low accuracy]. If the player makes one of these errors, gently correct them.' (2) Use getReviewWordsForNPC() from LanguageProgressTracker to get the review words. (3) Use the grammar patterns with lowest accuracy from the tracker. (4) Don't make corrections feel like a test — the NPC should correct naturally: 'Ah, you mean "je suis allé" not "je suis allée" — you're a man, so the past participle doesn't take an e!' (5) Track whether the NPC used the review words and whether the player responded using them — this counts as a spaced repetition review. (6) After conversation ends, if the player used review words correctly, update their mastery level and next review date.

tasks.title: Add text-related quest objectives: find, read, and complete comprehension quizzes
tasks.description: Create quest objectives that tie text collection into the broader quest system. (1) Add new canonical objective types to shared/quest-objective-types.ts: 'find_text' (locate and collect a specific text in the world), 'read_text' (read all pages of a collected text), 'complete_quiz' (answer all comprehension questions correctly for a text). (2) Add main quest objectives per chapter: Chapter 1 includes find_text for book 1; Chapter 2 includes find_text for books 2-3 and read_text for book 1; etc. Later chapters require reading earlier books. (3) Add side quests: 'The Bibliophile' — find and read 5 texts (any category); 'Book Critic' — complete 3 text quizzes with 100% accuracy; 'Literary Detective' — find all 12 of the writer's books; 'Recipe Collector' — find all 4 recipes; 'Letter Opener' — find and read 4 letters. (4) When a text is found, fire 'text_found' event on GameEventBus. When read, fire 'text_read'. When quiz completed, fire 'quiz_completed'. QuestCompletionEngine listens and updates objectives. (5) Text quest objectives should show minimap markers pointing to the text's spawn location (for find_text) or the Library menu hint (for read_text/complete_quiz). (6) The 'find_text' objectives should only activate when the chapter requires them — don't spoil later text locations early.

tasks.title: Fix existing Library notice content: remove filler, integrate with Texts system
tasks.description: The Library currently shows hardcoded SAMPLE_ARTICLES placeholder content. Fix by integrating with the new Texts system: (1) Remove the hardcoded SAMPLE_ARTICLES from BabylonNoticeBoardPanel.ts. (2) Convert the existing NoticeGenerator.ts template system to generate Text records using the new schema — each generated notice becomes a text with category 'flyer' or appropriate type. Store in the texts collection. (3) When a player visits a physical notice board in a settlement, load the settlement's texts with category 'flyer' from the API and display them. Mark as collected when read. (4) Apply the hover-to-translate treatment to notice text (same as books). (5) Persist which notices the player has collected and their quiz completion status using the ReadingProgress system. (6) Generate additional notice content as the game progresses: new notices appear on notice boards every few game-days (festival announcements, weather reports, local news), giving the player reasons to revisit. Create these as new Text records with isGenerated=true. (7) Some notices should tie into side quests — the existing questHook system in NoticeGenerator supports this; make sure it works with the new Texts schema.

tasks.title: End-to-end validation of main quest, library, texts, language progress, and asset scaling
tasks.description: Comprehensive integration test for all new systems. (1) Start a new playthrough in world 69bba18ce2e6d5c1cb3c7a9f. Verify the main quest Chapter 1 activates with the Missing Writer narrative. Verify the quest appears in both the Journal and the quest tracker. (2) Find a book text in the world (should be at a library/bookshop with correct 3D model and glow). Verify it can be collected with E and appears in Library with notification. (3) Open Library, verify categorized layout (Books/Journals/Letters/Flyers/Recipes tabs), click the book, read through pages verifying: NO inline translation by default, hover-to-translate works on individual words, vocabulary highlights are visually distinct. Complete the quiz, verify XP awarded and progress saved to server. (4) Close and reopen the game — verify Library progress, vocabulary, and grammar data persisted. (5) Check Language Progress page: verify vocabulary words from hover lookups appear, verify grammar patterns from NPC conversations appear with accuracy data. (6) Talk to main quest NPCs (editor, neighbor). Verify they reference the missing writer. Verify grammar feedback appears in chat. Verify vocabulary is tracked. (7) Complete Chapter 1 objectives. Verify chapter completion notification, bonus XP, and Chapter 2 activation. Verify Journal updates with investigation notes. (8) Open Admin Panel > Texts section. Verify texts are listed by category, can be edited, and the Generate button produces new content. (9) Open Admin Panel > Assets. Select a building asset. Verify scale controls appear with NPC reference preview. Adjust scale and verify it applies in-game. (10) Verify asset scaling fixes: check that the oversized food stand and giant leaf texture are now properly scaled. Write results to docs/MAIN_QUEST_INTEGRATION_RESULTS.md.
